Alaska · State Licensing Reference

Dietitian
Licensed Dietitian Guide

Issued by the Dietitians and Nutritionists Program under the Division of Corporations, Business and Professional Licensing. Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$150
$100 nonrefundable application fee + $50 initial license fee
Renewal
$125
Every 2 years (expires Dec 31 of odd-numbered years)
Processing Time
4 to 6 Weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
Up to 2 Years (depending on issue date relative to the cycle)
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Email

Credential Categories

Alaska Dietitian License Types

Licensed Dietitian

The credential required to practice dietetics in Alaska, overseen by the Division of Corporations, Business and Professional Licensing. The state also separately licenses 'Nutritionists'.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Alaska Licensed Dietitian

Complete Education & Supervised Practice

Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 1,000 hours of supervised dietetic internship.

Pass the CDR Examination

Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians.

Submit the Application via MY LICENSE

Complete the 'Application for Dietitian License' (Form #08-4400) online through the MY LICENSE self-service portal or via mail. Ensure your signature is notarized if applying via paper, and pay the $150 fee.

Provide Verification Documents

Arrange for the CDR to send official registration verification directly to the Alaska licensing office. Provide a recent passport-style photograph.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Official Application & PhotoApplication with notarized signature and a recent passport-style photograph.
  • CDR Registration VerificationMust be sent directly from the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
  • Verification of Out-of-State LicensesIf applying with existing licenses, verification from all other states must be submitted.
Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Provisional Rules

Alaska does not explicitly outline a temporary or provisional license for dietitians. You must complete the CDR exam and obtain full registration prior to licensure.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Alaska offers licensure by endorsement for those already licensed in another state. Applicants must demonstrate that their current state's requirements are substantially equivalent to Alaska's and must submit verifications for all current and past licenses.

Frequently Asked Questions

Alaska Dietitian FAQ

How much does an Alaska dietitian license cost?

The total initial cost is $150, which includes a $100 application fee and a $50 initial license fee.

When does the Alaska license need to be renewed?

Alaska dietitian licenses renew biennially and expire on December 31st of odd-numbered years. The renewal period requires a $125 fee. There is no grace period for practicing on a lapsed license.

Does Alaska require a background check?

While an extensive FBI fingerprint check is not centrally required for all applicants in this program, individuals must answer professional fitness questions and may need to provide court documents if they have a disciplinary history.
